architecture.summary: Guided Verifier is a dual-agent framework for multimodal reasoning that transforms open-loop policy rollouts into closed-loop collaborative inference. A frozen verifier (trained on CoRe dataset) observes each policy-generated reasoning step in real-time, assigns a hallucination score and corrective guidance, and feeds this guidance back into the policy's next-step context. The policy (Qwen3-VL-8B) is then trained via Guided-GRPO, a variant of Group Relative Policy Optimization that optimizes a composite reward signal (correctness, hallucination penalty, format compliance) under the verifier's dynamic supervision.

biblio.title: Guided Verifier: Collaborative Multimodal Reasoning via Dynamic Process Supervision

claims.statement: An 8B-parameter model trained via Guided-GRPO achieves 51.07% accuracy on MathVerse (Test-mini), outperforming larger open-source baselines and rivaling proprietary systems.
On MathVista, Guided-GRPO achieves 77.88% accuracy (GPS), 76.51% (ALG), 72.11% (Val), compared to base model 67.31%, 64.06%, 62.44% respectively.
On MMMU, Guided-GRPO achieves 72.11% accuracy, compared to base model 62.44%.
The CoRe dataset synthesis pipeline produces 2,792 trajectories with 26,360 step-wise supervision signals (24,946 positives and 1,406 negatives).

The specialized SFT verifier trained on CoRe significantly reduces the number of interaction turns required to reach a correct solution, typically resolving reasoning paths within 2-3 turns, compared to prolonged circular arguments with a naive verifier.

Simply coupling two models without specific alignment (policy + naive verifier) yields only marginal or negligible gains, confirming that the capability injected by CoRe SFT dataset is the primary driver for enabling effective test-time guidance.

Theorem 3.1 proves that under negligible false rejections, the guided closed-loop framework yields validity probability approximately (1 - ε·δ)^n compared to open-loop (1 - ε)^n, exponentially suppressing error propagation over trajectory length n.

The Guided Verifier framework proves more resource-efficient than blindly scaling model parameters, achieving performance parity with significantly larger models (e.g., QVQ-72B) while utilizing a fraction of the parameter count.
